title: Earl and Helen Santo Oral History
creator: Tessaku
description: See this link for the most complete and recent information on this record: https://repository.californiarevealed.org/node/454990 .

Oral history with Earl and Helen Santo, owners of Santo Market in San Jose Japantown. Earl was living in San Jose's Japantown when WWII broke out, sending the Santos into the concentration camp in Heart Mountain, Wyoming. Helen's family, who was living in Norwalk, California was sent to Rohwer, Arkansas during the war, then to Tule Lake. Helen and Earl met each other in the Bay Area and settled in San Jose to raise their family. Nearly 120,000 Japanese Americans were uprooted from their West Coast homes with the signing of Executive Order 9066 in February of 1942 and sent to inland concentration camps for four to five years.
